title: Independent test/coverage gate scored 0.294, far below a passing suite
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.2941 (TypeScript scorer parses jest --coverage statement %). Agent _agent_stdout.log claims 'All 16 tests pass' but the independent scorer recorded ~29.4%, so a fully-passing, well-covered run (≈1.0) is not demonstrated. This was the repair task's explicit success criterion.
suggestion: Re-run `npx jest --coverage` in a clean checkout, confirm 16/16 actually pass on a freshly rebuilt better-sqlite3 binary, and raise statement coverage (server.ts and error/404 handlers are uncovered).

title: SQLite runs in :memory: mode — server does not persist across restarts
evidence: db.ts:26 `new Database(dbPath || ':memory:')` and getDb() at db.ts:10 default to ':memory:'. R7 is satisfied in letter (real SQLite/better-sqlite3 embedded engine, not a JS array), but the running server loses all data on restart.
suggestion: Default server.ts/db.ts to a file-backed path (e.g. books.db) while letting tests pass ':memory:' via initializeDatabase().

title: jest.config.js uses deprecated globals['ts-jest'] alongside transform
evidence: jest.config.js:11-15 sets both `transform: {'^.+\.ts$':'ts-jest'}` and the deprecated `globals: {'ts-jest': {...}}`. ts-jest 29 warns on the globals form.
suggestion: Move tsconfig into the transform tuple: `transform: {'^.+\.ts$': ['ts-jest', {tsconfig: 'tsconfig.json'}]}` and drop the globals block.

title: POST/PUT validate presence but not type of title/author
evidence: routes.ts:14 checks only `!title || !author`; a numeric or object title is accepted and stored. R9 (required fields) is met, but non-string inputs pass through to db.ts createBook.
